title: Caching Systems (Part 1): Layers, Strategies, and Failure Modes
summary: Part 1 introduces cache layers, strategies, and failure modes so you can design caches you can trust.

title: Caching Systems (Part 2): Versioned Caches, Pub/Sub Invalidation, and Consistency
summary: Part 2 focuses on keeping caches correct: versioned keys, pub/sub invalidation, and practical consistency choices.

title: Designing a Notification Service using Python
summary: A guide on creating a Python-based notification service that integrates with multiple providers.

title: Gensim - Topic Modelling in Python
summary: Dive into topic modelling in Python using the Gensim library, and understand how it can help organize and understand large sets of textual data.

title: Django DRF-Spectacular: Documenting Custom Envelope Responses
summary: Learn why drf-spectacular misdocuments custom response envelopes in DRF and how to fix it with explicit or typed envelope serializers.

title: Django DRF-Spectacular Envelope Responses: Advanced Patterns and Trade-offs
summary: Explore advanced envelope response patterns in DRF and drf-spectacular, focusing on schema accuracy, error modeling, and pagination trade-offs.

title: Mastering the Adapter Design Pattern in Go
summary: The Adapter design pattern is a structural pattern that allows objects with incompatible interfaces to work together. In this article, we'll explore the Adapter pattern in depth, discussing its principles, benefits, and providing advanced examples in Go.

title: Caching Systems (Part 3): Building Cache-Aside in Go with Redis
summary: Part 3 implements a production-ready cache-aside layer in Go with Redis, TTL jitter, and safe invalidation.

title: A Comprehensive Dive into `go-jet`
summary: An in-depth exploration of go-jet, the powerful SQL builder for Go, and its pivotal role in efficient database management.

title: Integrating pprof with Gin
summary: A step-by-step guide on how to integrate Go's pprof with the Gin web framework using gin-contrib/pprof.

title: Observer Pattern with Goroutines: Building Signal-Like Mechanisms in Go
summary: Dive into Go's powerful concurrency features to implement the observer pattern, replicating signal-like mechanisms for event-driven programming.

title: Exploring the Option Struct Pattern in Go
summary: When working with the Go programming language, developers often encounter situations where functions may return values that could be missing or have a special meaning. The Option struct pattern is a powerful approach to address this, providing a clean and idiomatic way to handle optional values and errors. In this article, we'll delve into why and how Go uses the Option struct pattern.

title: Pelican: Static Site Generation in Python
summary: An introduction to Pelican, a powerful static site generator built on Python, and its various features and applications.

title: Unleashing Python's Hidden Power: Profiling for Performance Optimization
summary: Profiling is a fundamental technique in software development that enables programmers to identify and rectify performance bottlenecks in their code. Python offers several built-in and third-party profiling tools to help developers optimize their applications.

title: Python Metaprogramming and Decorators: Enhancing Code Dynamics
summary: Dive into Python's metaprogramming realm with a spotlight on decorators. Understand their functioning, unveil practical implementations, and craft custom decorators to uplift code aesthetics and reliability.

title: Implementing the Singleton Design Pattern in Go
summary: In software design, the Singleton pattern is a creational design pattern that ensures a class has only one instance and provides a global point of access to that instance.

title: Automated AsyncAPI Docs in Django: WebSocket Schemas Like Swagger
summary: Build Swagger-like AsyncAPI docs for Django WebSockets using decorators, Pydantic schemas, and a live HTML renderer.
